name: shinkoku
description: Japanese tax filing (kakuteishinkoku) AI agent plugin for Claude Code. Automates end-to-end income tax and consumption tax filing for sole proprietors and employees with side businesses. Supports bookkeeping, e-Tax input, furusato nozei, medical deductions, and housing loan deductions. Japan-resident, FY2025 tax code.

findings_summary.notes: Highly specialized Japanese tax filing plugin (MIT license, Feb 2026) with impressive breadth of coverage. Skills directory contains 19 purpose-specific skills: assess, capabilities, consumption-tax, e-tax, furusato, gather, income-tax, incorporation, invoice-system, journal, reading-deduction-cert, reading-invoice, reading-payment-statement, reading-receipt, reading-withholding, settlement, setup, submit, tax-advisor. Has a proper Python package in src/shinkoku/ with standard structure (cli/, config.py, db.py, models.py, tax_constants.py, tools/). The scripts/download_nta_specs.sh downloads official Japanese tax specification files (CABs) from e-tax.nta.go.jp — this is a legitimate data-download utility for offline use, not malicious. Has SECURITY.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, CHANGELOG.md, Makefile, pyproject.toml, uv.lock. pre-commit-config present. No dangerous patterns found. README includes clear disclaimer that users must verify output before filing and consult tax professionals. Concern: handles sensitive financial data — users should understand data stays local. Japan-only tax tool, not useful outside Japan.
